primary_accounting_issue: qualification IFRS 9 d’un actif financier dont les flux de trésorerie contractuels sont uniquement des remboursements de principal et d’intérêts, et identification des catégories de mesure possibles

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I selected IFRS 9 because it directly governs the classification and subsequent measurement of financial assets, including the solely payments of principal and interest condition, and no higher-precedence overlapping standard in the provided context addresses that same eligibility and measurement question.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low: the question maps directly to the IFRS 9 SPPI test and related measurement categories.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly define the SPPI condition and the resulting IFRS 9 measurement categories for financial assets.

peripheral_authority.reason: This document addresses cash flow statement classification and cash equivalents, not IFRS 9 classification of financial assets based on contractual cash flows.
This document addresses insurance contract accounting and only indirectly references IFRS 9 for separated components.
This document in the provided context addresses hedge accounting rather than the IFRS 9 SPPI-based classification and measurement of financial assets.

treatment_families.family: Debt instrument with SPPI cash flows held to collect contractual cash flows
Debt instrument with SPPI cash flows held both to collect contractual cash flows and to sell
Residual or designated fair value measurement for financial assets, including assets not measured under the SPPI business model categories or assets designated to eliminate an accounting mismatch

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats a financial asset with SPPI cash flows as measured at amortised cost when it is held within a hold-to-collect business model.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats a financial asset with SPPI cash flows as measured at fair value through other comprehensive income when the business model is both collecting contractual cash flows and selling.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 uses fair value through profit or loss as the residual category and also permits designation at fair value through profit or loss to eliminate or significantly reduce an accounting mismatch.
